reasoning: On May 11, Bibisara Assaubayeva's rating listed on Wikipedia is 2494. A 1% increase requires her rating to hit at least 2519 (2494 * 1.01 = 2518.94). FIDE ratings operate on a monthly release cycle, updating precisely on the 1st of every month, meaning Wikipedia's FIDE rankings for May 18 will identically reflect the May 1st rating of 2494. This yields a near-zero probability for resolving YES on May 18. By June, August, and November, new FIDE lists will be published. Recent news from mid-April 2025 states that she recently breached a live rating of 2509.3 and clinched her Grandmaster title. Given she is active (e.g., competing in the Women's Grand Prix and the Grenke festival in April), she has ample opportunities to gain the remaining ~10 rating points required to cross 2519. Doing so quickly by June is plausible but challenging; however, by November, a rising 21-year-old talent has a very realistic chance of accumulating the points needed. Therefore, uncertainty and probability increase significantly across the forecasted timeline.
